_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **October 2017** — Energy Sector Development Plan until 2030 approved by the Government of the Republic (in force as of October 2017). *Source: Ministry of Climate, https://kliimaministeerium.ee/en/energy-sector-development-plan.*
- **July 2023** — Ministry of the Environment renamed Ministry of Climate (Kliimaministeerium); policy mandate expanded to include transport, maritime affairs, and renewable energy coordination. *Source: Wikipedia / Ministry of Climate, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ministry_of_Climate.*
- **September 2024** — Ministry of Climate initiated legislative amendments to support offshore wind energy development; reverse auction for offshore wind announced for H1 2025. *Source: Ministry of Climate press release, https://kliimaministeerium.ee/en/news/ministry-climate-has-initiated-legislative-amendments-support-development-offshore-wind-energy.*
- **2024 (November–December)** — Public display and public discussion of the Strategic Environmental Assessment (SEA) report for ENMAK 2035. *Source: Ministry of Climate, https://kliimaministeerium.ee/en/energy-sector-development-plan.*
- **February 2025** — SEA report approved. *Source: Ministry of Climate, https://kliimaministeerium.ee/en/energy-sector-development-plan.*
- **April 2025** — SEA report declared compliant with the Environmental Impact Assessment and Environmental Management System Act by the minister. *Source: Ministry of Climate, https://kliimaministeerium.ee/en/energy-sector-development-plan.*
- **August 2025** — Estonia's most serious ASF outbreak in a decade: 125 wild boar and 10 domestic pig herds confirmed infected; 54,000+ pigs affected nationwide. *Source: Estonian World, https://estonianworld.com/business/estonia-faces-major-blow-as-african-swine-fever-hits-largest-pig-farm.*
- **October 2025 (Q4)** — ENMAK 2035 draft in government/parliament review. ASF emergency phase formally ended by Agriculture and Food Board (PTA) — disease-control measures continue. *Source: Ministry of Climate, https://kliimaministeerium.ee/en/energy-sector-development-plan; ERR News, https://news.err.ee/1609817265.*
- **January 2026** — ENMAK 2035 confirmed by Parliament (Riigikogu). Government approves Nuclear Energy and Safety Act (TEOS) draft and sends to Riigikogu. *Source: Ministry of Climate, https://kliimaministeerium.ee/en/news/estonian-government-approved-development-plan-make-energy-sector-more-reliable-affordable-and; Ministry of Climate news, https://kliimaministeerium.ee/en.*
- **January 2026** — Estonian agricultural ministry releases €75 million pig industry recovery plan; 2026 applications for state aid for farm construction to open. *Source: Pig Progress, https://www.pigprogress.net/health-nutrition/health/asf-estonia-multi-million-pig-industry-recovery-plan-launched.*
- **February 2026** — Estonia's Updated Recovery and Resilience Plan submitted/approved; REPowerEU investments confirmed (grid access €38M; biomethane €20.2M; decarbonisation reform €31.8M). *Source: European Commission, https://reforms-investments.ec.europa.eu/recovery-and-resilience-facility-1/country-pages/estonias-recovery-and-resilience-plan_en.*
- **May 2026 (approx.)** — Wind Speed Bonus programme introduced: municipalities eligible for up to €5 million in pre-construction wind energy funding. *Source: CEEnergy News, https://ceenergynews.com/renewables/estonia-fund-municipalities-wind-farm.*
- **August 2026** — Hard EU deadline for completion of all milestones and targets under Estonia's Recovery and Resilience Plan per EU RRF Regulation. *Source: European Commission, https://commission.europa.eu/business-economy-euro/economic-recovery/european-semester/estonias-recovery-and-resilience-plan_en.*
- **End 2029** — Target completion date for new onshore wind farms supported by reverse auction (~4 TWh). 12-year support period begins from completion. *Source: Ministry of Climate, https://kliimaministeerium.ee/en/news/ministry-climate-has-initiated-legislative-amendments-support-development-offshore-wind-energy.*
- **2030** — Target: 100% renewable electricity consumption on market basis; free and open electricity market; electricity prices projected at 6.6 cents/kWh (from current ~9 cents/kWh). *Source: ENMAK 2035, Ministry of Climate; taastuvenergeetika.ee, https://www.taastuvenergeetika.ee/en/renewable-energy-policy.*
- **End 2033 (at latest)** — New offshore wind energy to reach market under 20-year support scheme (€2.6 billion EC-approved state aid). *Source: Ministry of Climate; European Commission State Aid approval, https://ec.europa.eu/commission/presscorner/detail/en/ip_24_6262.*
- **2035** — ENMAK 2035 horizon: 80% of heat from renewables; renewable gas to reach one-third of gas consumption; energy system transition to clean economy. *Source: ENMAK 2035, Ministry of Climate, January 2026, https://kliimaministeerium.ee/en/news/estonian-government-approved-development-plan-make-energy-sector-more-reliable-affordable-and.*
- **2050** — Climate neutrality target; Climate-Resilient Economy Act to set binding sectoral GHG targets through to 2050. *Source: Ministry of Climate, https://kliimaministeerium.ee/en/climate-resilient-economy-act.*
